papers.title: Time to Healthy Lifestyle Habit Using Digital Health Tools in Adults at Risk of Diabetes
papers.abstract:
Status: NOT_YET_RECRUITING; Conditions: Prediabetes; Interventions: Hybrid digital intervention with personalized support., Standard lifestyle education; 1. Background \& Context

   The Challenge: Type 2 Diabetes (T2D) is a major public health burden. Lifestyle changes are effective, but little is known about how long it actually takes to make these changes stick.

   The Gap: Traditional trials look at outcomes at fixed time points (e.g., 12 weeks); Study Type: INTERVENTIONAL

papers.title: Assessing the Impact of Perceptions of Unpredictability on Objective Measures of Food Consumption and Metabolism
papers.abstract:
Status: NOT_YET_RECRUITING; Conditions: Healthy Volunteer, Obestity; Background:

Many people in the United States are overweight or obese. This natural history study will look into how life events during childhood can impact eating behaviors and weight gain as adults.

Objective:

To explore how childhood experiences affect adult eating behaviors.

papers.abstract:
Status: NOT_YET_RECRUITING; Conditions: Insulin Resistance, Obesity; Interventions: Alpha-lipoic acid, Mirabegron, Placebo; Background:

Obesity and related illnesses cause at least 2.8 million deaths each year worldwide. Few treatments exist for obesity that are safe and widely available. A study drug (mirabegron \[MG\]) combined with a supplement (alpha-lipoic acid \[ALA\]) may help.

papers.abstract:
Status: RECRUITING; Conditions: Healthy Volunteer; Interventions: Urolithin A, Placebo; Background:

As people age, the cells in the pancreas that produce insulin begin to release less of this hormone, and levels of blood glucose (sugar) rise. This can lead to illnesses such as diabetes.
